question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /network/status endpoint with concurrent unauthenticated HTTP requests, triggering repeated calls to RetrieveGenesis() and RetrieveLatest() that exhaust the database connection pool, causing all subsequent transaction confirmation queries to time out and producing a total network shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of /network/status requests with very short context deadlines, causing repeated context cancellations inside RetrieveGenesis() and RetrieveLatest() that leave dangling database transactions open, eventually exhausting the DB connection pool and halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential dependency of RetrieveGenesis() followed by RetrieveLatest() followed by addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() in NetworkStatus() by sending requests that cause each step to hold a DB connection open simultaneously, multiplying connection consumption and starving the transaction pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the ErrNodeIsStarting error path in NetworkStatus() by flooding requests before the genesis block is initialized, causing all Rosetta clients to treat the node as permanently starting and stop submitting transactions, effectively shutting down transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the IsOnline() check in NetworkStatus() — which only tests whether blockRepo is non-nil — by sending requests that cause blockRepo operations to panic or return nil blocks, bypassing the online check and returning corrupt network state that causes downstream clients to halt transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send malformed or oversized MetadataRequest bodies to /network/status, causing the Rosetta SDK router to allocate unbounded memory during deserialization, leading to OOM and crashing the service, which stops all transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkStatus() calls addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() on every request — which executes the latestNodeServiceEndpoints SQL query — to cause repeated full table scans on address_book_entry, saturating I/O and blocking transaction-related DB queries from completing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the ErrDatabaseError return path in addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() by flooding /network/status requests, causing the database to return errors that propagate as retriable errors to Rosetta clients, which then retry indefinitely and amplify load until transaction confirmation collapses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the peers.ToRosetta() call in NetworkStatus() — which iterates over all address book entries — by causing the address book to contain an extremely large number of entries (via a separate injection path), making each /network/status response allocation so large that it causes GC pressure and service degradation halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the currentBlock.GetTimestampMillis() integer division (ConsensusStartNanos / int64(time.Millisecond)) in the NetworkStatus() response path to produce a zero or negative timestamp that causes Rosetta clients to reject the network status as invalid and cease submitting transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that force RetrieveLatest() to return a block whose Index is less than genesisBlock.Index, causing NetworkStatus() to propagate ErrBlockNotFound to all callers and making the network appear unavailable, halting transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sync.Once in initGenesisRecordFile() — which is called transitively by RetrieveGenesis() inside NetworkStatus() — by racing concurrent requests so that a failed genesis initialization is permanently cached via once.Do(), causing all subsequent NetworkStatus() calls to return ErrNodeIsStarting forever and shutting down transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that initGenesisRecordFile() uses a sentinel value of genesisConsensusStartUnset = -1 to detect uninitialized state, by causing a DB error on the first call so that genesisBlock.ConsensusStart remains -1 and every subsequent NetworkStatus() call re-attempts and fails the DB query, creating a persistent denial-of-service on the genesis initialization path?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent /network/status requests that each independently call initGenesisRecordFile() before once.Do() fires, causing multiple goroutines to execute the selectGenesis SQL query simultaneously, creating a thundering herd on the database that starves transaction-processing queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the deferred cancel() calls in RetrieveLatest() and RetrieveGenesis() — which cancel the DB context — by timing requests so that context cancellation fires while a DB query is mid-execution, causing partial reads that corrupt the block state returned in NetworkStatus() and mislead clients into halting transaction submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /network/options endpoint with concurrent requests, causing repeated iteration over types.GetTransactionResults() and allocation of operationStatuses slices, leading to excessive GC pressure that degrades the service and prevents transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that NetworkOptions() builds operationStatuses by iterating over the transactionResults map — which is a shared global — without any locking, causing a data race if another goroutine modifies the map, leading to a runtime panic that crashes the service and halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause NetworkOptions() to return an empty or incorrect OperationStatuses list — by exploiting the GetTransactionResults() returning a nil or empty map — causing Rosetta clients to reject all transaction types as unsupported and halt transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the errors.Errors global slice — which is populated at package init time and returned directly in NetworkOptions() Allow.Errors — by causing concurrent reads and writes to the slice (e.g., via AddErrorDetails appending), triggering a data race that panics the service and shuts down transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted /network/options request that causes persistence.IsTransactionResultSuccessful() to be called with an unexpected result code, returning an incorrect Successful flag for a transaction status, causing Rosetta clients to misclassify transaction outcomes and stop confirming transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the n.operationTypes slice — which is built once in NewNetworkAPIService() and shared across all requests — by triggering a concurrent read/write race if any code path appends to it, causing a runtime panic that crashes the service and halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause NetworkOptions() to return a NetworkOptionsResponse with a nil Version pointer — if n.version was not properly initialized — causing a nil pointer dereference in the Rosetta SDK serialization layer that panics the service and stops transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the OperationTypeFee constant being appended to operationTypes in NewNetworkAPIService() — making it appear as a valid operation type — by crafting transactions that use FEE as an operation type, causing the network to process malformed transactions that corrupt state and halt confirmation of legitimate transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the /network/list endpoint with concurrent requests, exploiting the fact that it returns a shared n.network pointer without copying, causing a data race if any other goroutine modifies the NetworkIdentifier, leading to a panic that crashes the service and halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: NetworkList()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to /network/list that cause the Rosetta SDK to allocate a new NetworkListResponse on every call, and by flooding with thousands of concurrent requests, exhaust heap memory and trigger OOM, crashing the service and stopping all transaction confirmation?
